description: Japanese - Raijin is god of thunder and lightning in japanese mythology.

historicalContext.historicalPeriod: Kojiki and Nihon Shoki (early references); Developed in Heian period
historicalContext.sourceText: Raijin imagery elaborated in Heian Buddhist texts and art

shrineSites.name: Kasuga Taisha
shrineSites.significance: Associated with thunder and weather deities

buddhistEquivalents.connection: Direct adoption - retains name and iconography

historicalNotes: Elaborated in Heian period art and literature
Iconography: drum, naked man, aggressive posture
Often paired with Fujin (wind) - natural forces pairing
Represents human desire to control natural forces

keyMyths.title: Raijin's Capture by Sugawara no Michizane
keyMyths.description: Sugawara no Michizane, wrongly exiled, caused thunderstorms to plague Kyoto as a form of vengeance. Raijin, the cause, was eventually captured and subdued by Michizane's spiritual power, leading to Michizane's deification as Tenjin, the god of scholarship and thunder.

keyMyths.title: Raijin and the Navel Stealing
keyMyths.description: Raijin is said to steal the navels of children, a belief that led to parents instructing their children to cover their bellies during thunderstorms. This practice was meant to protect them from Raijin's mischievous or malicious intent.

keyMyths.title: Raijin's Drumming
keyMyths.description: Raijin creates thunder by beating on his drums, usually depicted as a ring of drums surrounding him. The intensity and frequency of his drumming directly correlate to the strength and duration of the thunderstorms.

keyMyths.title: Collaboration with Fujin
keyMyths.description: Raijin is often depicted alongside Fujin, the god of wind. Together, they control the weather, with Fujin unleashing winds and Raijin responding with thunder and lightning, causing storms.

keyMyths.title: Raijin's Appearance
keyMyths.description: Raijin is often depicted as a fierce, demonic-looking figure with wild hair, sharp claws, and a muscular physique. His intimidating appearance reflects his power over the natural world and the fear he inspires.
